title: 12 Selling Unmatured Marketable Bonds (Government of Canada and Treasury Bills)
body_text:
12	Selling Unmatured Marketable Bonds (Government of Canada and Treasury Bills)
12.1	Sending Unmatured Marketable Bonds on Collection
Submit Unmatured marketable bonds for sale to Central 1 on a collection basis. Bonds will be sold at the market value. This applies to Government of Canada Bonds and Treasury Bills. Coupons from these bonds cannot be sold separately.
Proceed as follows to send Unmatured marketable bonds on collection.
1.
Obtain the bondholder's signature on the application for redemption. If the bondholder is deceased, obtain the signature of each executor or administrator.
2.
Obtain a Power of Attorney from the bondholder. If the bond itself does not contain a Power of Attorney section, complete Power of Attorney to Sell and Transfer Securities, Form 3788, and attach it to the bond. Bearer Bonds do not require a Power of Attorney.
Note:	The Registered Owner(s) must sign the Power of Attorney exactly as the bond is registered or the bond will be returned.
If the bondholder is deceased, the phrase 芒聙聹Signature of transferor and authority to sign guaranteed芒聙聺 must be included in the signature guarantor space of the Power of Attorney form to authenticate the guarantor or administrator.
3.
Ensure the bondholder芒聙聶s signature or executors芒聙聶 or administrators芒聙聶 signatures are guaranteed by a credit union signing officer authorized according to the list on file with Central 1.
Note:	Bonds must be sent to Central 1 for an over-guarantee of the credit union芒聙聶s signature. See Section 4.4, Guarantee and Over-Guarantee of Signatures.
Related Information
Power of Attorney to Sell and Transfer Securities,
Form 3788
12.2	Preparing the Deposit for Central 1
At the end of the business day, proceed as follows.
1.
Complete a Notification of Bond Sale, Form 1323, describing the bond for sale.
2.
Enclose the following documents in an envelope:
Bond
Power of Attorney to Sell and Transfer Securities, Form 3788 (as required)
Original copy of Notification of Bond Sale, Form 1323
3.
Forward to Central 1, as per internal credit union transportation policy by courier, at the following address:
Central 1 Credit Union
Payment Services
Attention: Tracing
1441 Creekside Drive
Vancouver, BC V6J 4S7
Related Information
Notification of Bond Sale,
Form 1323
12.3	Settlement and Adjustments for Unmatured Marketable Bonds
Central 1 will settle all marketable bonds sold (when settlement has been received) daily through the credit union芒聙聶s Central 1 Canadian Dollar current account. Central 1 settles for the amount in the redemption value box.
If the incorrect amount is written in the redemption value box, the credit union will not be credited immediately for the correct amount. The Bank of Canada will pick up the error and process the required adjustment, but this may take up to a month. The minimum amount for adjustments must be greater than $6.99. The Bank of Canada will not process adjustments on amounts of $6.99 or less.
A photocopy of the sale transaction will be returned to the credit union. A copy of the sale is required for your member to report income for CRA.
Note:	When a marketable bond is sold, Central 1 won芒聙聶t know what the receiving credit amount for it will be. At best, it芒聙聶ll be the market rate for what a person will pay for the bond. This has no bearing on the adjustments for regular Canada Savings Bonds adjustments. This also doesn芒聙聶t pass through the Bank of Canada when a bond is sold.
END
